title: Battle of Plassey
provisions: Between Siraj ud-Daula and British (Robert Clive)
Coup by Mir Jafar against Siraj ud-Daula
Pre-battle: Treaty of Alinagar signed
Mir Jafar became Nawab after victory
Mir Jafar paid 26 lakh to British
Ghaseti Begum's wealth restored
Causes: No peshkash, asylum to Krishna Das in Fort William, mounting of guns, Black Hole Tragedy (Holwell)

title: Battle of Buxar
provisions: Between British (Hector Munro) and combined forces of Mir Qasim, Shah Alam II, Shuja ud-Daula of Awadh
Resulted in Treaty of Allahabad (1765)
Shuja ud-Daula ordered to pay 50 lakh
Shah Alam II granted Diwani rights of Bihar, Bengal, Orissa to British
Mir Jafar reinstated as Nawab
Mir Qasim exiled
Established British as supreme power in Bengal

title: 1st Carnatic War
provisions: Extension of War of Austrian Succession in Europe
Dupleix (French) attacked Madras
Battle of Ambur: French killed Nawab Anwaruddin
First instance of European-trained Indian troops defeating a larger Indian army
Ended with Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle
British got Madras back
French got Cape Breton Island and Nova Scotia

title: 2nd Carnatic War
provisions: Succession dispute in Hyderabad and Carnatic
Muzaffar Jung and Chanda Saheb supported by French (Dupleix)
Nazeer Jung and Mohammed Ali supported by British
Siege of Arcot: Robert Clive captured Carnatic capital
Dupleix recalled to France
Mohammed Ali established as Nawab of Carnatic

title: 3rd Carnatic War
provisions: Extension of Seven Years' War in Europe
Conte de Lally led French forces
Sir Eyre Coote led British forces
Battle of Wandiwash: Decisive British victory
Ended with Treaty of Paris (1763)
French allowed to retain territories but effectively finished as a power in India
French restricted to trading posts only

title: 1st Anglo-Mysore War
provisions: Between Haider Ali of Mysore and British
Initial Maratha attack on Mysore
Haider Ali defeated British at Battle of Pollilur
Haider Ali used Mysorian rockets against British
Ended with Treaty of Madras
Mutual restoration of conquered territories

title: 2nd Anglo-Mysore War
provisions: Haider Ali gave asylum to French at Mahe
British attacked Mahe provoking Haider Ali
British, Maratha, Nizam joined against Mysore
Haider Ali died during the war
Tipu Sultan continued the war after Haider Ali's death
Ended with Treaty of Mangalore
Prisoners exchanged and territories restored

title: 3rd Anglo-Mysore War
provisions: Tipu Sultan attacked Travancore (British ally)
British, Maratha, Nizam formed triple alliance against Tipu
Mir Sadiq acted as British informant within Tipu's court
Ended with Treaty of Seringapatnam
Tipu paid 3.3 crore as war indemnity
Half of Mysore annexed: Baramahal, Malabar, Dindigal
Tipu Sultan's sons held as hostages by British

title: 4th Anglo-Mysore War
provisions: Lord Wellesley was Governor General
Wellesley offered Subsidiary Alliance to Tipu Sultan who refused
Tipu Sultan was member of Jacobin Club
Tipu planted Tree of Liberty at Seringapatnam
Tipu had relations with Napoleon
Tipu Sultan died fighting in this war
Wodeyar dynasty restored as rulers of Mysore
Tipu introduced new calendar system
Tipu released coin with Lord Venkateswara's image
Tipu had connections with Shringeri Sharadapeetham

title: 1st Anglo-Maratha War
provisions: War of succession among Marathas
Raghunath Rao sought British help to become Peshwa
Bombay unit of British refused to accept Warren Hastings' orders
Treaty of Purandar signed during the war
Ended with Treaty of Salbai
British retained Broach and Salsette
No protection given to Raghunath Rao
No French territory allowed in Maratha empire

title: 2nd Anglo-Maratha War
provisions: Nana Fadnavis granted port to French provoking British
Peshwa Bajirao II signed Treaty of Bassein (Subsidiary Alliance)
Scindia and Bhonsale refused Subsidiary Alliance
Battle of Assaye: British defeated Scindia
Battle of Laswari: British victory
Scindia defeated but retained Gwalior
Bhonsale defeated and signed Subsidiary Alliance

title: 3rd Anglo-Maratha War
provisions: Pindaris joined Peshwa Bajirao II against British
Peshwa Bajirao II's dominion completely annexed
Peshwaship abolished permanently
Holkar joined Bajirao II but remained largely undefeated
Bajirao II given small territory at Satara
Sangola Agreement signed
12-administrator system introduced
Saranjam (temporary land grants) converted to Watan (permanent) for 5 Maratha houses

title: 3rd Battle of Panipat
provisions: Between Marathas and Ahmad Shah Abdali (with Rohillas and Shuja ud-Daula)
Fought during Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao's tenure
Sadasiva Rao Bhau killed in battle
Vishwas Rao killed in battle
Shamsheer Bahadur killed by swivel gun
Marathas suffered devastating defeat
Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao died of heart attack upon hearing news of defeat
Ended Maratha ambition of pan-India dominance

title: 1st Anglo-Sikh War
provisions: British army positioned on banks of River Sutlej
Ended with Treaty of Lahore
Half area of Punjab annexed by British
British resident appointed to interfere in Sikh affairs
Rani Jindan sold Kashmir to Gulab Singh
1 crore war indemnity imposed on Sikhs
Duleep Singh was a minor ruler

title: 2nd Anglo-Sikh War
provisions: Mulraj (governor of Multan) revolted against British
Battle of Chillianwala fought during this war
Khalsa Army defeated and reduced to half
Remaining Khalsa forces joined British
Punjab completely annexed by British
Power exercised by mother Rani Jindan on behalf of minor Duleep Singh
Court officials turned against Rani Jindan
End of Sikh sovereignty

title: Nadir Shah's Invasion
provisions: During reign of Mohammed Shah (Rangeela)
Battle of Karnal fought
Nadir Shah looted Peacock Throne from Delhi
Nadir Shah looted Kohinoor diamond
Massive plunder of Delhi
Exposed extreme weakness of Mughal Empire

title: Battle of Bedara/Hooghly
provisions: Between Dutch and British in Bengal
Dutch defeated by British
Dutch withdrew from India after this defeat
Dutch had tried to collude with Mir Jafar
Ended Dutch ambitions in India
Dutch retreated to Indonesia

title: Ahmad Shah Abdali's Invasions
provisions: Multiple invasions of India
Mohammed Shah (Rangeela) lost Multan and Punjab to Abdali
Abdali's agent expelled by Marathas during Alamgir II's reign
Marathas captured Punjab from Abdali's agents under Balaji Baji Rao
Culminated in 3rd Battle of Panipat (1761)
Allied with Rohillas and Shuja ud-Daula against Marathas
Shah Alam II exiled for 12 years due to instability caused by invasions

title: Battle of Ambur
provisions: Part of 1st Carnatic War
French killed Nawab Anwaruddin of Carnatic
First significant victory of European-trained troops against Indian forces
Demonstrated superiority of European military tactics in India
Chanda Saheb installed as Nawab with French support

title: Siege of Arcot
provisions: Part of 2nd Carnatic War
Robert Clive captured Arcot, capital of Carnatic
Clive held Arcot with small force against larger army
Turned the tide of 2nd Carnatic War in favour of British
Established Robert Clive's military reputation
Led to eventual defeat of French-backed Chanda Saheb

title: Battle of Wandiwash
provisions: Part of 3rd Carnatic War
Between British (Sir Eyre Coote) and French (Conte de Lally)
Decisive British victory
Effectively ended French military power in India
Led to Treaty of Paris (1763)
French reduced to mere trading presence in India

title: Battle of Pollilur
provisions: Part of 1st/2nd Anglo-Mysore War
Haider Ali defeated British forces
Haider Ali used Mysorian rockets effectively
One of the worst British defeats in India
Demonstrated Mysore's military capability against European forces

title: Battle of Assaye
provisions: Part of 2nd Anglo-Maratha War
Between British (Arthur Wellesley) and Scindia's forces
British defeated Scindia
Hard-fought battle with heavy British casualties
Led to Scindia accepting Subsidiary Alliance

title: Battle of Laswari
provisions: Part of 2nd Anglo-Maratha War
British victory against Scindia's forces
Complemented Battle of Assaye in breaking Scindia's power
Led to final submission of Scindia to British terms

title: Battle of Chillianwala
provisions: Part of 2nd Anglo-Sikh War
Fought in Punjab
Fierce battle with heavy casualties on both sides
Khalsa Army put up strong resistance
Eventually led to complete annexation of Punjab

title: Battle of Karnal
provisions: Between Nadir Shah of Persia and Mughal Emperor Mohammed Shah (Rangeela)
During Ahmad Shah's reign / Nadir Shah's invasion
Mughals decisively defeated
Led to plunder of Delhi
Nadir Shah took Peacock Throne and Kohinoor diamond

title: Battle of Colachel (1741)
provisions: One of the earliest decisive defeats of a European colonial power by an Indian kingdom.
Fought between the Kingdom of Travancore under Marthanda Varma and the Dutch East India Company.
French support went to Travancore, contradicting claims of French backing the Dutch.
Led to the Treaty of Mavelikkara (1753), limiting Dutch influence.
